findings.title: Transaction commit overwrites whole tables (lost update under concurrency)
findings.description: The in-memory transaction clones the db, runs the handler on the draft, then commits by wholesale reassignment: `db.cartItems = draft.cartItems; db.orders = draft.orders; db.products = draft.products` (L54-56). Two transactions that interleave (each cloning the same base, then committing) would clobber each other's writes — last-writer-wins on the entire table rather than row-level merge. The handlers here are synchronous so no interleaving occurs within a single event-loop turn, and the tutorial uses per-request in-memory stubs, so this is not exploitable as wired; noting it as a latent correctness footgun in the reference transaction pattern. The scanner's missing-await flags on L62/L65/L69 are false positives: Array.push and Map.set are synchronous.
findings.recommendation: For any persistence behind this interface, use real transactional isolation (or row/version-level merge) rather than swapping whole table references on commit. No action needed for the in-memory tutorial stub.
